info.title: Legalesign API
info.description:
Legalesign.com is an electronic signature software-as-a-service. This is the documentation for its REST API. 

Start by creating a trial account at https://app1.legalesign.com/auth/intro/. Upon account activation, select the API development option and get an API key. You will be in sandbox mode that will limit you to 5 signers. Add them on https://app1.legalesign.com/acc/settings/#api  .

For any queries contact us at support@legalesign.com.

post.description: Returns a redirect response (302) with link in the Location header to a one-use temporary URL you can redirect to, to see a preview of the signing page. Follow the redirect immediately since it expires after a few seconds.

footer.description: HTML only. Final PDF footer text

footer_height.description: HTML only. Final PDF footer height

header.description: HTML only. Header for final pdf

header_height.description: HTML only. Header height for final pdf

signature_placement.description: HTML docs only. 1 = place on each page, 2= place at end of doc

text.description: If HTML document, return raw html

auto_archive.description: Send document archive very soon after signing.

cc_emails.description: who will be cc'd  with sender on email notification when signed

download_final.description: Final PDF is available to download

uuid.description: Object ID alone not in URI format

url.description: the url where you wish to get notified

notify_when.description: the type of callback to receive, value must be 'all' or 'signed'

text.description: Raw html. Data must contain either text or templatepdf or template.

templatepdf.description: Resource URI of templatepdf object. Data must contain either text or templatepdf or template.

template.description: Resource URI of text template object. Data must contain either text or templatepdf or template.

append_pdf.description: Append Legalesign validation info to final PDF. If not included uses the group default.

auto_archive.description: Send to archive soon after signing. Keeps web interface clutter free

do_email.description: Use Legalesign email to send notification emails

cc_emails.description: Comma delimited list of email addresses that are notified of signing or rejection.

footer.description: HTML based doc only. The footer for the final pdf. Use keyword \"default\" to use group default footer.

footer_height.description: HTML based doc only. Pixel height of PDF footer, if used. 1px = 0.025cm

header.description: HTML based doc only. The header for the final pdf. Use keyword \"default\" to use group header footer.

header_height.description: HTML based doc only. Pixel height of PDF footer, if used. 1px = 0.025cm

pdftext.description: Assign values to PDF sender fields, use field labels as keys. Ensure document fields lables are unique.

return_signer_links.description: Return document links for signers in the response BODY.

signers_in_order.description: Notify signers in their order sequence. If false all are notified simulataneously/

user.description: Assign document another user in the group.

pdf_password.description: Assign a password to the final PDF, requires pdf_password_type set at group level, or using attribute

pdf_password_type.description: If using pdf_password. Not needed if set at group level. 1 = Password is kept by us. 2 = Password is deleted after shown to first signer.

message.description: use keyword \"default\" to use group message

DocumentSignerResponse.description: will be ordered list of 8 signer values as follows - str signer resource_uri, str first name, str last name, str email, str behalf of,  bool fields complete, int signer status, int signer order (zero-indexed). For status see SignerStatusEnum.

pdfheader.description: Set to true to use group default

xframe_allow_pdf_edit.description: allow xframe for pdf edit page (beta feature)

members.description: list of members using their user resource_uri

user.description: assign to group member if not api user

ax.description: left vertical, 0 = left page edge, 1 = right page edge

ay.description: upper horizontal, 0 = top page edge, 1 = bottom page edge

bx.description: right vertical, 0 = left page edge, 1 = right page edge

by.description: lower horizontal. 0 = top page edge, 1 = bottom page edge

element_type.description: Must be one of the following, * Signature - signature field * Initials - initials field * Text - signer field (field for signer to complete) * Admin - sender field (field to complete by admin user when sending)

fieldorder.description: ordering of fields as signer progresses

label.description: help signer/sender understand what to do

page.description: which page to place field on

align.description: one of the following * 1 - left * 2 - middle * 3 - right

optional.description: set true to allow field to be ignored

substantive.description: set if field substantive to contract terms, if so will not let other sign till this field completed

password.description: if not set a verification email is sent. at least 6 chars with a digit

redirect_to.description: if verification link is emailed (password is not set) then person is redirected to legalesign

groups.description: comma delimited list of groups to add user to, can be full group resource_uri or groupId

permission.description: set user permissions * 1 - admin * 2 - create and send docs, team user * 3 - readonly, team user * 4 - send only, team user * 5 - send only, individual user * 6 - create and send docs, invidual user

UserPost.description: Create a new user who can gain access to your groups. You should specify the groups the user should be able to access. Only the groups the API user can access are valid. If you do not set a password then a verification link will be emailed to the user. This will allow the user to set their password and activate their account. In this case you can also add a redirect_to url to redirect the user back to your website after they have activated their account. N.B. Make sure you add the user to at least one of your groups, otherwise you will not have API access to the user information.

PDFFieldValidationEnum.description: select validation (e.g. date, email, or number) or fields type (e.g. checkbox) * 1 - Email * 2 - yyyy/mm/dd * 3 - yy/mm/dd * 4 - dd/mm/yyyy * 5 - dd/mm/yy * 6 - mm/dd/yy * 7 - mm/dd/yy * 8 - yyyy.mm.dd * 9 - yy.mm.dd * 10 - dd.mm.yyyy * 11 - dd.mm.yy * 12 - mm.dd.yyyy * 13 - mm.dd.yy * 14 - yyyy-mm-dd * 15 - yy-mm-dd * 16 - dd-mm-yyyy * 17 - dd-mm-yy * 18 - mm-dd-yyyy * 19 - mm-dd-yy * 20 - Dropdown list. see options attribute * 21 - Yes/No * 24 - Checkbox tick/cross * 25 - Checkbox tick/blank * 26 - Checkbox cross/blank * 30 - yyyy/mm/dd (automatic) * 31 - yy/mm/dd (automatic) * 32 - dd/yy/yyyy (automatic) * 32 - dd/mm/yyyy (automatic) * 33 - dd/mm/yy (automatic) * 34 - mm/dd/yyyy (automatic) * 35 - mm/dd/yy (automatic) * 36 - yyyy.mm.dd (automatic) * 37 - yy.mm.dd (automatic) * 38 - dd.mm.yyyy (automatic) * 39 - dd.mm.yy (automatic) * 40 - mm.dd.yyyy (automatic) * 41 - mm.dd.yy (automatic) * 42 - yyyy-mm-dd (automatic) * 43 - yy-mm-dd (automatic) * 44 - dd-mm-yyyy (automatic) * 45 - dd-mm-yy (automatic) * 46 - mm-dd-yyyy (automatic) * 47 - mm-dd-yy (automatic) * 48 - d mmmmm yyyy (automatic) * 50 - Whole number * 51 - Number * 52 - Currency (2 decimals) * 53 - 1 number * 54 - 2 numbers * 55 - 3 numbers * 56 - 4 numbers * 57 - 5 numbers * 58 - 6 numbers * 59 - 7 numbers * 60 - 8 numbers * 61 - 9 numbers * 62 - 10 numbers * 63 - 11 numbers * 64 - 12 numbers * 65 - 1 characters (any text) * 66 - 2 characters (any text) * 67 - 3 characters (any text) * 68 - 4 characters (any text) * 69 - 5 characters (any text) * 70 - 6 characters (any text) * 71 - 7 characters (any text) * 72 - 8 characters (any text) * 73 - secret code, add code in options * 74 - file attach, append to email to signer * 75 - file attach, append to final PDF

DocumentStatusEnum.description: document status is represented as follows, * 10 - Initial state, check signer status for sent/unsent * 20 - Fields completed * 30 - Signed * 40 - Removed (before signing) * 50 - Rejected

SignerStatusEnum.description: signer status is represented as follows, * 5 - unsent/scheduled * 10 - sent * 20 - email opened * 30 - fields complete * 40 - signed * 50 - downloaded * 60 - rejected
